summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones the vulnerable n8n tag, injects an integration proof, and demonstrates that a read-only sharee can overwrite stored OAuth token data for a shared credential through the reconnect callback.
expected_security_impact: A user who can only read a shared credential should still be able to drive the OAuth reconnect flow and replace the stored oauthTokenData and accountIdentifier with attacker-controlled values.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned n8n@2.18.0 at commit 6336f0a447eab3600b1fe13216f1abd5b6e25dff, built the reproduction image, executed the injected Jest proof, and produced `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS credential=9R8BNzSP2Mzy2c0d member=adb2e22d-2c64-48d2-bd82-f5145db190cd overwritten_access_token=attacker-access-token` after verifying that the stored credential had been rewritten.
verification_method: The containerized verifier installs the repository, injects a single integration test, runs the real OAuth2 auth and callback controllers against a local token server, then decrypts the stored credential and checks for the attacker-controlled access token and account identifier. The run succeeds only if the exact EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS proof string appears in the test output.
evidence_produced_by_run: Jest reported PASS for test/integration/controllers/oauth/readonly-shared-oauth-reconnect.poc.test.ts inside the containerized checkout.
reproduction/verify.sh confirmed the proof string with `[evohunt] verified proof string in test output`, and the host run completed with `[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the public n8n repository on the host, checks out tag n8n@2.18.0, verifies commit 6336f0a447eab3600b1fe13216f1abd5b6e25dff, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
